operation: First, a workplane is initialized on the XY plane, and a cylinder with a height of 5 units and radius of 10 units is extruded. Next, the top face of the cylinder (positive Z direction face) is selected to create a new workplane. A polar array of 6 points is defined on this workplane, with a pattern radius of 8 units, spanning 360 degrees starting at 0 degrees. Circles with 1 unit radius are drawn at each array point, followed by a through-all cut operation to create 6 complete through holes running the full height of the cylinder.
geometry: The model is a short solid right circular cylinder, with six identical uniform-diameter through holes passing entirely through its height. The holes are arranged in an evenly spaced circular pattern that is concentric to the main cylinder's central axis, parallel to the axis, and positioned between the cylinder center and its outer circumferential edge.
function: This is a symmetric mechanical mounting flange or spacer, designed to be secured via its evenly spaced through holes for assembly applications, providing stable, symmetric support, alignment, or spacing between connected components in mechanical systems.
